name: Baked Samosa Logs with Fruit-Sweetened Tomato
recipeIngredient: Baby peas (1 1/3 cups), fresh or frozen
Cayenne pepper (optional, 1/8 tsp) or Paprika (1/2 tsp)
Olive oil spray or olive oil (for brushing)
recipeInstructions:
Logs:
1. Steam cauliflower in a large pan for 10 minutes.
2. If using frozen peas, defrost; add peas and steam for an additional 3 minutes.
3. Transfer the mixture of steamed cauliflower and peas into a bowl.
4. Add coconut, curry powder, cayenne or paprika, cilantro, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well.
5. Allow the mixture to cool slightly, then divide into 12 portions (about 1/2 cup each).
6. Place one chapati on a work surface and lightly brush with water.
7. Shape one portion of the vegetable mixture into a log on the chapati.
8. Fold in the sides about 1 1/2 inches from the bottom edge, then roll the chapati into a log shape.
9. Place the log seam side down on a baking sheet.
10. Repeat with remaining chapatis.
11. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly spray or brush samosas with olive oil.
12. Bake for 15-20 minutes, until crisp and golden brown. Cut each samosa in half.
Chutney:
1. Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add ginger, jalapeno pepper, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds; cook until fragrant (about 1-2 minutes).
3. Add tomato puree, diced tomatoes with juice, white grape juice, salt, and pepper to taste.
4. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 35-40 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and allow chutney to cool slightly before serving.
Hints:
- Samosas can be made ahead of time and baked just before serving.
- If you cannot find chapatis, substitute flour tortillas (white or whole wheat).
- Chutney can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated.
- When doubling the recipe, increase baking time by 15 minutes.
- If freezing chutney, season with salt and pepper before serving.
